creator: wxs@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue, Apr 28, 2009 at 06:07:05PM +0100, Tony Finch wrote:
> On Mon, 27 Apr 2009, Wesley Shields wrote:
> 
> > I can't seem to reproduce this.  With an empty MANPATH I'm able to view
> > the documentation using "git help tag" or "git help <foo>".
> 
> Below is a script of me demonstrating the problem. I wonder if you have a
> symlink from /usr/local/share/man to /usr/local/man ?

I don't have a symlink from /usr/local/share/man to /usr/local/man.

> $ pkg_info -I 'git*'
> git-1.6.0.1         Distributed source code management tool

This is likely the problem. Looking back at the cvs logs for the port I
recall that I had fixed a similar bug but that I introduced the same bug
in a later update, only to catch it later and fix it again. Based upon
the version you're running it appears that you have the one with the
bug. Can you please update to a newer version and let me know if the
problem persists?

-- WXS
